query: Venezuela January 2026 Absolute Resolve Maduro arraignment New York narco-terrorism charges DOJ indictment details

mako.raw:
{"query":"Venezuela January 2026 Absolute Resolve Maduro arraignment New York narco-terrorism charges DOJ indictment details","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1591,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/LSB11401","title":"The United States’ Prosecution of Nicolás Maduro Moros: United States v. Maduro | Congress.gov | Library of Congress","content":"Following the seizure of Nicolás Maduro Moros (Maduro) by U.S. armed forces in Caracas, Venezuela, on January 3, 2026, a superseding indictment charging him and other defendants with narco-terrorism, narcotics, and weapons offenses was unsealed in the U.S.
The indictment charges Maduro, Rondon, and Chacin with narco-terrorism conspiracy in violation of 21 U.S.C. § 960a, and all five defendants are charged with conspiracy to import cocaine into the United States and weapons possession offenses.
The specific acts that the indictment alleges the defendants took in furtherance of the narco-terrorism and drug trafficking conspiracies include providing drug traffickers with diplomatic passports, private planes under diplomatic cover, and armed military escorts."},{"url":"https://abc7.com/post/ousted-venezuelan-president-nicolas-maduro-appear-manhattan-federal-court-monday-arraignment-alleged-drug-charges/18351236/","title":"Ousted Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro to appear in Manhattan federal court on Monday for arraignment on alleged drug charges - ABC7 Los Angeles","content":"CARACAS, Venezuela -- Ousted Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro will appear in federal court in New York on Monday for arraignment on a four-count indictment that charged him with leading a 25-year narco-terrorism conspiracy.
After arriving at Stewart Air National Guard Base in Newburgh, New York, Maduro was taken by helicopter to New York City and then to the Metropolitan Detention Center in Brooklyn."},{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/maduro-arraigned-on-us-narco-terrorism-charges/","title":"Maduro arraigned on US narco-terrorism charges | Courthouse News Service","content":"U.S. prosecutors unsealed a superseding indictment against Maduro and his wife, accusing them of an international cocaine trafficking conspiracy that involved terrorist groups armed with machine guns.
“Nicolas Maduro Moros, the defendant, now sits atop a corrupt, illegitimate government, that for decades has leveraged government power to protect and promote illegal activity, including drug trafficking,” prosecutors say in the indictment.
Maduro’s wife Cilia Adela Flores De Maduro also pleaded “not guilty, completely innocent" Monday to the charges she faces."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prosecution_of_Nicol%C3%A1s_Maduro_and_Cilia_Flores","title":"Prosecution of Nicolás Maduro and Cilia Flores - Wikipedia","content":"Full case name — United States v. Nicolás Maduro Moros, Diosdado Cabello Rondón, Ramón Rodríguez Chacín, Cilia Adela Flores de Maduro, Nicolás Ernesto Maduro Guerra and Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores
Maduro and Flores were transported to the United States and taken into federal custody in New York City, and are currently detained at the Metropolitan Detention Center in Brooklyn.
SDNY — Nicolás Maduro Moros — Former President of Venezuela — Accused of leading conspiracy with FARC; authorizing cocaine trafficking routes — 50,000,000 — Deposed and captured, sent to U.S. (2026); pleaded not guilty (2026); ongoing trial
On January 3, 2026, during U.S. military strikes on Venezuelan targets as part of Operation Absolute Resolve, U.S. special forces captured Nicolás Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, at a residence in Caracas.
The National Assembly, dominated by Maduro loyalists, also issued a formal resolution condemning the U.S. decision.
Democratic leaders did not comment directly on the $50 million reward, but had previously emphasized support for Venezuelans during the January 2025 increase to $25 million under former President Biden.
"AG Bondi offers $50M for arrest of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro".
"DOJ doubles reward for arrest of Venezuela's Maduro to $50M"."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Venezuela January 2026 Absolute Resolve Maduro arraignment New York narco-terrorism charges DOJ indictment details","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prosecution_of_Nicol%C3%A1s_Maduro_and_Cilia_Flores","title":"Prosecution of Nicolás Maduro and Cilia Flores - Wikipedia","content":"U.S. criminal case against Nicolás Maduro and his associates. Not to be confused with United States of America v. | Court | United States District Court for the Southern District of New York |. Carvajal-Barrios***, is a federal criminal case filed against former president of Venezuela Nicolás Maduro, and various senior Venezuelan officials by the United States Department of Justice (DOJ) in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York in 2011 and unsealed on March 26, 2020. The indictment initially included Hugo Carvajal, who was arrested in Spain in 2021 and extradited to the United States on a warrant for drug trafficking in 2014. On January 3, 2026, a superseding indictment was unsealed after Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, were captured during a U.S. military operation in Caracas supported by the DOJ and Central Intelligence Agency (CIA).","score":0.7040996,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/26/nicolas-maduro-federal-court-narco-terrorism-case","title":"Nicolás Maduro appears again in New York court on ‘narco-terrorism’ charges | Nicolás Maduro | The Guardian","content":"Nicolas Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, are seen in handcuffs after landing at a Manhattan helipad, escorted by heavily armed federal agents in January. Deposed Venezuelan president and his wife, who both pleaded not guilty, were captured by US military in January. The deposed Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro appeared in a Manhattan federal court on Thursday for his “narco-terrorism” case after his capture by US military forces earlier this year. The hearing opened with the defense and prosecution arguing over whether Maduro should be allowed to use Venezuelan government funds to pay for his defense. The defense has insisted that the US is violating the deposed leader’s constitutional rights by blocking government money from being used for his legal costs. US special forces captured Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, on 3 January in a controversial pre-dawn raid during an assault on Caracas that reportedly killed 100 people.","score":0.6450534,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.justice.gov/usao-sdny/pr/manhattan-us-attorney-announces-narco-terrorism-charges-against-nicolas-maduro-current","title":"Manhattan U.S. Attorney Announces Narco-Terrorism Charges Against Nicolas Maduro, Current And Former Venezuelan Officials, And Farc Leadership","content":"An official website of the United States government. A **.gov** website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Image 6: United States Department of Justice SealU.S. Department of Justice. U.S. Department of Justice. U.S. Attorney's Office, Southern District of New York. Berman, the United States Attorney for the Southern District of New York, Brian Benczkowski, Assistant Attorney General for the Criminal Division of the Department of Justice, Uttam Dhillon, Acting Administrator of the United States Drug Enforcement Administration (“DEA”), and Alysa D. One Superseding Indictment includes narco-terrorism, drug trafficking, and weapons charges against NICOLÁS MADURO MOROS, Diosdado CABELLO RONDÓN, HUGO ARMANDO CARVAJAL BARRIOS, a/k/a “El Pollo,” CLÍVER ANTONIO ALCALÁ CORDONES, LUCIANO MARÍN ARANGO, a/k/a “Ivan Marquez,” and SEUXIS PAUCIS HERNÁNDEZ SOLARTE, a/k/a “Jesús Santrich.” The other Superseding Indictment alleges violations of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (“IEEPA”) and the Foreign Narcotics Kingpin Designation Act (“Kingpin Act”), and a related conspiracy to defraud the U.S. Department of the Treasury, the Office of Foreign Assets Control (“OFAC”), against TARECK ZAIDAN EL AISSAMI MADDAH, JOSELIT RAMIREZ CAMACHO, and SAMARK LOPEZ BELLO.","score":0.6150191,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/maduro-arraigned-on-us-narco-terrorism-charges","title":"Maduro arraigned on US narco-terrorism charges","content":"U.S. prosecutors unsealed a superseding indictment against Maduro and his wife, accusing them of an international cocaine trafficking conspiracy","score":0.5996498,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/LSB11401","title":"The United States' Prosecution of Nicolás Maduro Moros","content":"The indictment charges Maduro, Rondon, and Chacin with narco-terrorism conspiracy in violation of 21 U.S.C. § 960a, and all five defendants","score":0.5863576,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.93,"request_id":"923b4d79-2583-4b9d-8d84-0c33bbc8afcf"}
